definition: The singleness or gathered unification of mind associated with steadiness, samādhi, and jhāna.
notes: The project promotes this entry to a major rule-bearing record because ekaggatā is too important to leave as a thin definitional stub. The project keeps singleness as the default because it matches the literal force and supports the house preference for samādhi as unification of mind rather than concentration. Unification can be used when the relation to samādhi needs to be foregrounded. One-pointedness remains acceptable in technical discussion, but it should not drive the lexicon toward a narrow concentration model when the broader gathered quality of mind is what matters. The OSF glossary confirms singleness as the house default while preserving unification and one-pointedness as technical alternates.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and meditative contexts
When the relation to samādhi as unification of mind needs to be explicit
In technical comparison with standard Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Use only when the technical continuity is useful and the line will not drift into concentration-language.
Useful when plain singleness needs a little more practical texture.

example_phrases.notes: Closely related phrase that helps explain the family meaning.

example_phrases.translation: a unified mind that is single
example_phrases.notes: Shows the close relation of ekaggatā and samādhi.

version_notes: Promoted from a minor stub to a rule-bearing major entry with explicit guidance for singleness, unification, and one-pointedness.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping singleness as the house default in samadhi-adjacent analysis while preserving unification and one-pointedness as technical alternates.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'singleness' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'concentration' or other unrecorded alternates.
